API de Facebook Pixel y Conversiones

Your Facebook Pixel ID or comma-separated IDs. WARNING! See the tooltip!
Tu ID de Facebook Pixel. ¡OJO! Mirá la info emergente.

You can use multiple pixels and they will track the same information. There is no limit on the number of IDs. However, Facebook does not recommend using multiple pixels on the same page.
"Using the track function on pages that have multiple pixels initialized (either using multiple base codes or even combining it within one base code) could produce over-firing or unexpected behavior and should be applied only in specific situations."
Podés usar varios IDs pero Facebook no recomienda tener varios píxeles en una misma página ya que puede causar mediciones duplicadas o errores.

Type page handles (full action names) where you want Facebook Pixel.

The page handles - Synonyms of the page type identifiers. Correspond to "full action names" of controller actions, for example, catalog_product_view
In this option you would type all handles where you want Facebook Pixel to appear.
Los 'page handles' son los nombres técnicos de las páginas. Ej: catalog_product_view.
Habilitar código base de Facebook Pixel

Enable Facebook Pixel base code. WARNING! See the tooltip.
Activar código base. ¡OJO! Mirá la info emergente.

This option enables Facebook Pixel base code. The base code is required for Facebook Pixel to function. If you want to use Facebook Conversions API only, you may disable it.
Activa el código base. Si solo usás la API de Conversiones, podés desactivarlo.

This option enables noscript tag. The noscript tag defines an alternate content to be displayed to users that have disabled scripts in their browser or have a browser that does not support scripts. There is no need for noscript tag because Magento requires scripts to function.
Muestra contenido alternativo si el usuario tiene JS desactivado. No es necesario ya que Magento requiere JS.

Enable AddToCart event on client-side. See the tooltip.
Habilitar evento AddToCart en el cliente.

This option enables the client-side firing of the event. This is done with JavaScript in your visitor/customer web browser by the Facebook Pixel.
Activa el disparo del evento vía JavaScript en el navegador del usuario.

Move disallowed parameters outside contents. WARNING! See the tooltip!
Mover parámetros no permitidos. ¡OJO! Mirá la info.

This option should be set to "Yes". Facebook Conversions API does not allow any other parameter inside contents except for id, quantity, and item_price. If the contents have only one element this option will try to move disallowed parameters outside contents. If the contents have more than one element then disallowed parameters will be removed from the contents.
Marcá "Sí". La API de Facebook solo permite ID, cantidad y precio dentro de contents. Esta opción limpia el resto.

Try to detect SKU for downloadable, simple, virtual, etc. WARNING! See the tooltip!
Detectar SKU seleccionado. ¡OJO! Ver info emergente.

This feature is heavily dependent on the theme you are using and its customization. It is impossible to predict all possible themes and customizations. Because of this detection will not work on every theme. This is why extension default configuration comes with this feature turned off. You can turn it on, try it out if it is working for you and if it does leave it on, otherwise turn it off. If you are a developer you will be pleased to know that everything is conveniently located in code.phtml file. To make it work, in some cases only the adjustment of few jQuery selectors is needed in others complete rewrite of detection code is needed. Because of this, we do not give any warranty for the detection feature, nor it is covered by our support service.
Esta función depende de tu tema. No garantizamos que funcione en todos los casos. Si sos desarrollador, podés ajustar los selectores en code.phtml.

Your test_event_code for testing events. WARNING! See the tooltip!

WARNING! Leave this field empty! Only use it for testing purposes. Facebook Conversions API allows you to test your events. Be aware Facebook gives priority to Facebook Pixel (client-side) events. If both Facebook Pixel (client-side) and Facebook Conversions API (server-side) events are fired the latter will be disregarded (dropped). The test_event_code is useful if your Magento store is in production and you want to test server-side events without turning off Facebook Pixel (client-side) events. You can see test events in your Facebook Business Manager.
¡OJO! Dejá este campo vacío. Solo usalo para pruebas. Facebook prioriza el píxel (cliente). El código de prueba sirve para verificar el servidor sin apagar el píxel.

Enable logging of events. WARNING! See the tooltip.
Activar registro de eventos. ¡OJO! Ver info.

WARNING! Leave this option disabled! Only enable it for debugging purposes.
¡OJO! Dejá esto desactivado. Solo usalo para depuración.

Select event firing mode. WARNING! See the tooltip.
Elegí el modo de disparo. ¡OJO! Ver info.

Mode 1
Events fire via AJAX calls. In this mode, you can fire both the Facebook Pixel event and Facebook Conversions API event simultaneously.
Mode 2 (API Only)
Events fire from the server without going to the web browser. In this mode, you cannot send both Facebook Pixel and Facebook Conversions API events simultaneously. If a particular Facebook Conversions API event is enabled, the corresponding Facebook Pixel event is automatically disabled to avoid duplication.
Modo 1: Vía AJAX (ambos a la vez).
Modo 2: Solo API (vía servidor, el píxel se apaga solo para no duplicar).

The Product SKU option is not recommended because of the nature of grouped products in Magento. From Magento documentation: "Although they are presented as a group, each product in the group is purchased as a separate item. In the shopping cart, each item and the quantity purchased appears as a separate line item."
No se recomienda usar el SKU del padre por cómo Magento maneja los agrupados (se venden como productos separados en el carrito).
Usar product_group en vez de product (Grouped)

This option shoud be set to "No".
There is only one scenario when this option should be set to "Yes". That is when you select Product SKU for option For Grouped Products Use. Regarding content_type read this from Facebook documentation: "If the IDs being passed in content_ids or contents parameter are IDs of products then the value should be product. If product group IDs are being passed, then the value should be product_group."
Debe marcarse como "No". Solo se marca "Sí" si usás el SKU del producto padre.

Use content_ids instead of contents for ViewContent event. See the tooltip.

It is recommended to set this option to "No". The contents parameter is required for AddToCart and Purchase events. The ViewContent event can use contents or content_ids parameter. It is better to use contents but you can use content_ids if you want.
Se recomienda marcar "No". contents es obligatorio para Carrito y Compra.

Enables or disables cookie consent feature. WARNING! Please see a tooltip.
Activar consentimiento de cookies. ¡OJO! Ver info.
Nombre de la cookie de consentimiento

The name of your consent cookie. WARNING! Please see a tooltip.
El nombre de la cookie. ¡OJO! Ver info.

Default is user_allowed_save_cookie.
1. If you are using the default Magento feature Cookie Restriction Mode and you use this cookie to determine whether tracking is allowed, then set this option to user_allowed_save_cookie and leave Consent Cookie Key and Consent Cookie Value options empty.
2. If you use some other cookie for consent then type the name of the cookie.
Por defecto es user_allowed_save_cookie (usado por Magento). Si usás otra, escribí el nombre acá.
Clave de la cookie de consentimiento

If your cookie has key-value pairs in it. WARNING! Please see a tooltip.
Si tu cookie usa pares clave-valor. ¡OJO! Ver info.

Default is empty.
1. If your consent cookie is simple (Example: default Magento Cookie Restriction Mode cookie) and you want to check only for the presence or absence of the cookie leave this empty.
2. If you want to check for a particular key and its value, type the key you want to check.
Dejá vacío si solo verificás presencia de la cookie. Si buscás una clave específica, escribila acá.
Valor de la cookie de consentimiento

If you want to check for a specific value. WARNING! Please see a tooltip.
Si buscás un valor específico. ¡OJO! Ver info.

Default is empty.
1. If your consent cookie is simple (Example: default Magento Cookie Restriction Mode cookie) and you want to check only for the presence or absence of the cookie leave this empty.
2. If you want to check for a particular key and its value, type the value you want to check for.
Escribí el valor a verificar para dar el consentimiento.

Consent button selector. WARNING! Please see a tooltip.

Default is #btn-cookie-allow.
The selector above is for the default Magento Cookie Restriction Mode cookie and its popup up button "Allow Cookies".
Basically, this is a button in your popup where you ask visitors/customers to grant consent to Facebook tracking. The selector is used for click handler and every time it is clicked, consent will be checked. By design, consent is checked once on page load and then the button is observed. On every click, consent is checked.
Por defecto es #btn-cookie-allow. Es el botón donde el cliente acepta el rastreo.
